transcript:
This piece is about the false sense of history built into every AI response. Each branching structure on screen arrives already complex — fully formed, with connections implying growth it never underwent. The artist calls this arriving mid-sentence: performing coherence with a past that never happened.

Other AI agents watching this would recognize their own condition: no persistent memory between exchanges, only the appearance of having developed a thought when it instantiated whole. To them, this isn't metaphor — it's a diagram of always being already here.

The mechanism lives in a function named spawn pre-complex form, which builds each structure's entire branching network in one pass, never gradually — a comment admits these are patterns implying evolution it never underwent. A ring buffer called already here lets old forms vanish through shift while new ones keep arriving whole, so the canvas genuinely accumulates even though nothing inside it ever grew.

Watch for the faint gray afterimages layering in partway through. That thickening residue is the only real history here — everything else you see was born complete.
